title: rhaphiolepis × daduheensis
intro: Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis, also known as Rhaphiolepis hybrid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a hybrid species derived from Rhaphiolepis umbellata, a plant native to China and India, and is used in traditional Chinese medicine. Rhaphiolepis × daduheensis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion.
